Fern Polrrrcal PRACrrcEs CouvrrssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
March 26,2007
Donald E. Wilson, Consultant
Senate Committee on Veterans Affairs
1020 N Street, Ste. 251
Sacramento, CA 9581 4-4900
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-07-039
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the staternent of
economic interests provisions of the political Reform Act (the..A"i).,
QUESTION
Are you required to report the business venture you entered into in Jarruary 2007
on your combined 2006 awrual/leaving office Statement of Economic Interests (po.-
700)?
CONCLUSION
No. Since your combined annual/leavingoffice Form 700 conectly covers the
period ending December 3I,2006, you are not required to report the businlss venture you
entered into in January 2007. You will disclose tlie investment on your next annual Form
700 or if you are required to file an assuming or leaving office statement during ZOO7.
FACTS
You are a consultant for the California Senate Comrnittee on Veterans Affairs and
a Trustee for the Center Unified School District. You previously sat on the Antelope
community Planning Advisory council (cpAC) for sacramento county. In our
telephone conversation on March 13,2007, you indicated that you resigned from the
Antelope CPAC effective December 26,2006. on January 24:2007,yiu filed a Form
700 as a combined leaving office statement for your AntelLpe CpAC fosition and an
annual statement for your Senate and school board positions.
I Govemm".rt Code sections 81000 - glll4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109 - 18997, of the California Code of Regulations.


FileNo. A-07-039
Page No. 2
Section 87300 requires that all state and local government agencies adopt a
conflict-of-interest code. sect ion 87302 requires that ihe code speci"fy the positions in the
agency that involve the making orparticipation in the making of a""isionsiatmay
foreseeably have amaterial effect on any financial interest. lndividuals (designated
employees) holding these positions are iequired to file periodic Statements of Economic
Interests (Form 700).
Designated employees are required to file Form 700 within 30 days of assuming a
position designated in_their ug"t "y'sionflict-of-interest codg, an annual statement for
each year that they hold the position Qsya!1due on ap.il I of the followingye ar), and a
Ieaving office statement to be filed *ithin sit days urtJi.uirg office. rmp-loyees with
multiple filing obligation-s may comprete one "expanded statement,, covering the
disclosure requirem-ents for all positions. (Section gn}z;Regulation rsi:0";- rne
combined Form 700 you filed on January 24,2007,"oo"rtly covered the period ending
December 31,2006, for all three of thepositions yo" rrera during 2006. rirolror", yo,,
are not yet required to report an investment that was acquired in2007. This investment
must be disclosed on your next required Form 700.
If you have any other questions, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
ANALYSIS
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

CONCLUSION: No. Since your combined annual/leavingoffice Form 700 conectly covers the
